steps.title: Match the verb series
steps.content: The sentence lists what the temperature does across three months: it is 62.6 degrees in June, then increases in July, and then dips in August. The blank must be a finite simple-present verb that is parallel with "is" and "dips."

steps.title: Remove tense and form errors
steps.content: A uses the present perfect, which breaks the simple-present pattern. C is a participle, so it cannot serve as the main verb in the series. D shifts to the past tense even though the surrounding verbs are present. The answer is B.
